paper_title: Tackling Discrepancies in Trade Data: The Harvard Growth Lab International Trade Datasets
research_answer: For long-run analysis going back to the 1970s, SITC Revision 2 is the better choice in terms of temporal coverage, spanning 1976 to 2023 — nearly a half-century of data. HS1992, by contrast, only covers 1995 to 2023, giving you less than three decades. The trade-off is in product granularity: HS1992 offers six-digit product-level detail, which is considerably more granular than SITC's four-digit level. For economic complexity analysis, finer product granularity generally means more precise measurement of export baskets and comparative advantage. So researchers face a classic tension: SITC gives you longer time series for tracking structural transformation and complexity trends, while HS1992 gives you sharper product-level resolution for more recent periods. The choice should be driven by whether temporal depth or product specificity matters more for the research question at hand.
supporting_quotes: SITC Revision 2 (Standard International Trade Classification): Features four-digit product-level granularity covering 1976–2023.
HS1992 (Harmonized System, 1992 Vintage): Features six-digit product-level granularity covering 1995–2023.
